title: Nickelate lever-stacking strain mechanism decomposition
question: Why does compressive epitaxial strain enhance La3Ni2O7 Tc, and why does phonon-only Tc plateau at ~26 K?
mechanism_summary: Compressive strain increases c/a ratio, which enhances Ni-dz2/O-pz/Ni-dz2 sigma-bonding across the bilayer. This has two effects: (1) increased N(E_F) on the sigma-bonding Fermi surface sheet (+25% from 0% to -2% strain), enhancing phonon coupling; (2) hardening of the apical breathing phonon mode, which couples strongly to the sigma-bonding electrons. The phonon-only Tc increases from 17.4 K (0%) to 21.9 K (-2%) for La3Ni2O7 and to 26.3 K with Sm substitution (-2%). However, this saturates because the phonon lambda is limited to ~1.0 by the frequency range of the relevant modes. The dominant pairing channel (spin fluctuations) requires separate treatment.

unstrained.note: At 0% strain, dx2-y2 dominates (cuprate-like). dz2 sigma-bonding present but not dominant.

strained_minus1pct.note: Strain increases dz2 weight by ~15%. Sigma-bonding band widens.

strained_minus2pct.note: At -2%, dz2 and dx2-y2 become comparable. Sigma-bonding fully developed. This is the regime where bilayer nickelates become distinct from cuprates.

dz2_orbital_mechanism.trend: Monotonic increase in dz2 weight with compressive strain: 32% -> 37% -> 40% for 0% -> -1% -> -2%

apical_breathing_mode.description: Out-of-phase displacement of apical O between the two NiO2 planes of the bilayer
apical_breathing_mode.coupling_to_dz2: Strong: mode modulates Ni-O_apical bond length, directly affecting dz2-pz hybridization

Sm_vs_La.mechanism: Smaller Sm ion compresses the La-site cage, increasing c/a and dz2 overlap. Also slightly hardens phonons (higher omega_log), contributing to Tc enhancement.

N_EF_vs_strain.trend: N(E_F) increases by ~15% from 0% to -2% strain, primarily from sigma-bonding sheet expansion

lambda_decomposition.trend: Strain primarily enhances lambda_sigma (dz2 channel) by ~57%, while lambda_other increases modestly (~19%)

RPA_estimate_lambda_sf.note: Spin fluctuations in La3Ni2O7 arise from Ni-dz2 interplane superexchange (J_perp ~ 30-60 meV, Sakakibara et al. PRL 2024). Compressive strain enhances J_perp by improving dz2 overlap.

spin_fluctuation_estimate.expected_Tc_with_sf_K: 40-90 (highly uncertain; depends on symmetry of sf-mediated gap)
spin_fluctuation_estimate.note: Key question: do phonon (s-wave) and spin-fluctuation (s+/- or d-wave) channels cooperate or compete? In nickelates, sigma-bonding geometry favors s+/- pairing where phonons and sf can add constructively.

vs_SLAO_onset_40K.note: Phonon-only is 55% of onset Tc on SLAO substrate

vs_pressurized_96K.note: Phonon-only is 27% of pressurized zero-resistance Tc (different conditions)

phonon_fraction_estimate.note: Large uncertainty due to onset vs zero-resistance ambiguity and pressure difference. Central estimate: phonon contributes ~45% of nickelate pairing, with spin fluctuations providing the remainder.

limiting_factors: lambda_phonon saturates at ~1.0: adding more phonon coupling from strain/substitution pushes into strong-coupling regime where Tc ~ omega_log/1.2 * exp(-1.04(1+lambda)/lambda) saturates
omega_log ~ 300 K is set by Ni-O bond stiffness and cannot be increased much by strain alone
Mu* = 0.10 is already optimistic; realistic Coulomb repulsion limits phonon-only Tc
Isotropic Eliashberg ignores gap anisotropy that could enhance Tc by 10-30% for sign-changing order parameters
why_phonon_Tc_plateaus_at_26K.ceiling: Phonon-only Tc ceiling for bilayer nickelates is ~25-35 K (lambda ~ 0.8-1.2, omega_log ~ 280-320 K). This is fundamental to the phonon frequency scale of oxide materials.

key_insight: Strain works by enhancing dz2 sigma-bonding, which increases both the phonon coupling (lambda_sigma) and the spin-fluctuation nesting. The phonon channel provides ~30-70% of the total pairing (best estimate ~45%). To reach 80 K or above, the spin-fluctuation contribution is essential. DMFT + Eliashberg or functional RG calculations that include both channels simultaneously are needed to make quantitative predictions.
